title: Arkanian Revolution

The '''Arkanian Revolution''' was a fierce and bloody [[civil war]] fought on [[Arkania/Legends|Arkania]] in [[50 BBY/Legends|50 BBY]] between the [[Planet/Legends|planet's]] [[Arkanian Dominion|Dominion]] government and the [[Arkanian Renegades]]. Known for their genetic and [[Cybernetics/Legends|cybernetic]] expertise, Arkanian scientists began to experiment on the unintelligent local [[Yaka]] [[Species/Legends|species]], despite the objections of a group of more socially conscious scientists. After several centuries of injustice, these scientists formed a faction known as the Arkanian Renegades, and launched a coup against the Dominion, after forming a powerful army of [[Cyborg/Legends|cyborg]] [[Assimilator]]<ref name="swcw">''[[SkyeWalkers: A Clone Wars Story]]''</ref> warriors. In the process of fighting what they saw as injustices to the Yaka, the Arkanian Renegades became much like their opponents by committing similar atrocities.

The Renegades' army, whose number included [[Gorm (cyborg)|Gorm the Dissolver]], butchered the Dominion's forces in early skirmishes. The desperate Dominion appealed to the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Republic]] and [[Jedi Order/Legends|Jedi Order]] to intervene on their behalf. Republic police squads were eventually deployed to Arkania, supplemented by [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]] forces including [[Mace Windu/Legends|Mace Windu]], [[Aqinos]], and the [[Iron Knight]]s. The Renegades were soundly defeated by the reinforcements, and those not killed in battle were executed for their [[Treason/Legends|treason]]. Some of their cyborg creations, including Gorm the Dissolver, survived, becoming [[Bounty hunter/Legends|bounty hunters]] and hired killers. The Arkanian Revolution, although unsuccessful, contributed greatly to the escalating public fear of [[Droid/Legends|droids]].

==Prelude==
[[File:Arkanian.jpg|thumb|left|90px|An Arkanian geneticist]]
Based on their adopted [[Homeworld/Legends|homeworld]] of [[Arkania/Legends|Arkania]] in the [[Colonies/Legends|Colonies]], the [[Arkanian/Legends|Arkanian]] [[Species/Legends|species]] considered themselves the pinnacle of evolution, and were one of the most advanced species in [[The galaxy/Legends|the galaxy]] by the time of the formation of the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]]. Known for their scientific and medical breakthroughs, the Arkanians were keen geneticists, eager to carry out any genetic modification research, even that which the Republic had outlawed. One of the earliest and best known experiments performed by Arkanian scientists was when they relocated a group of [[Xexto/Legends|Xexto]] from their homeworld of [[Troiken/Legends|Troiken]] to the [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim]] world of [[Quermia/Legends|Quermia]], in order to perform genetic modifications on the species in a secluded and out-of-the-way environment. The experiment was cut short when the Arkanians ultimately decided to abandon the project out of fear of Republic repurcussions, leaving the newly-altered Xexto to develop on their own, eventually becoming the [[Quermian/Legends|Quermian]] species.<ref name="negas">''[[The New Essential Guide to Alien Species]]''</ref>

[[File:Yaka_EGTA.jpg|thumb|right|100px|The Arkanians made severe modifications to the Yakas.]]
Thousands of years later, in [[490 BBY]], the Arkanians discovered the [[Yaka]] species in the nearby [[Yaka system/Legends|Yaka system]]. The Arkanians considered the Yakas feebleminded and weak, and took it upon themselves to use their knowledge of genetics and technology to improve the species' intelligence, implanting high-tech [[Cyborg/Legends|cyborg]] brains into the Yakas. Some Arkanians were distressed by their compatriots' actions, considering them to be morally questionable, but their objections were ignored. The Yakas adapted quickly to their alterations, developing mental abilities considered to be at a genius level. However, the cyberetic implants caused an apparent loss of emotion in the modified Yaka, along with a twisted, often cruel sense of humor.<ref name="egtas" /> The Arkanians' genetic experiments were considered by some portions of galactic society to be some of the greatest atrocities ever performed.<ref name="uaa">''[[Ultimate Alien Anthology]]''</ref>

==Revolution==
===Conflict===
Several centuries later, the issue came to the forefront some [[50 BBY/Legends|fifty years]] before the [[Battle of Yavin/Legends|Battle of Yavin]]. A faction calling themselves the [[Arkanian Renegades]] intensified the objections to the still-ongoing modification of the Yakas, but the [[Arkanian Dominion|Dominion]] government once more turned a blind eye to the issues being raised and continued to fit Yaka offspring with enhancements.<ref name="egtas" /><ref name="nec" />

[[File:Gorm.jpg|thumb|left|90px|Gorm would ultimately become the most famous of the Renegades' creations.]]
The Renegades decided to take matters into their own hands and formulated a plan to launch a coup and overthrow the Dominion, establishing a new order. Like their political opponents, the Renegades possessed an intimate knowledge of genetics and technology, and they opted to use this expertise to their advantage. They set about building a spliced-together, patchwork army, ultimately performing experiments not unlike those they opposed. Using arcane and forbidden practices, they constructed a half-mechanical, half-organic [[Mercenary/Legends|mercenary]] army, created with parts of various different [[Alien/Legends|alien]] species and [[Droid/Legends|droids]], who possessed a determined, warlike attitude. This army would fight the Renegades' conflict, and they were confident it would topple the Dominion.<ref name="egtd">''[[The Essential Guide to Droids]]''</ref>

The army—whose number included [[Gorm (cyborg)|Gorm]], a formidable warrior who would achieve much greater notoriety in later decades—proved almost invincible against the forces of the Arkanian Dominion in combat. The [[Cyborg/Legends|cyborgs]], called the Assimilators, slaughtered thousands of pro-Yaka-modification Arkanians, and the Renegades soon had the upper hand over their helpless opposition. 

[[File:ZetaMagnus.jpg|thumb|right|90px|Zeta Magnus was the prime clone of the Accelerated Transgenic Heuristic Abhorers.]]
To combat the Renegade's Assimilator army, the [[techno-lord]]s of the Arkanian Dominion developed their own [[Cloning/Legends|clone]] army of bioengineered cyborg monstrosities. The [[Accelerated Transgenic Heuristic Abhorer]]s were created by splicing the genetic sequences of their Arkanian creators with that of the Yaka, [[Rakghoul]]s, [[Amorphiian]]s, [[Dianoga/Legends|dianoga]], and other abominable species. They stood at four meters tall, regenerated quickly, possessed accelerated strength, [[noological implant]]s that granted them genius-level intelligence, were synthesized to lustfully seek out prey and destroy them with relish, and could digest nearly any material both organic and inorganic. Once the Dominion's Abhorers were pitted against the Renegade's Assimilators, their savage, violent, calculated, and sadistic fighting drew the revolution to a stalemate.<ref name="swcw">''[[SkyeWalkers: A Clone Wars Story]]''</ref> The desperate Dominion eventually pleaded<ref name="nec" /> for the Republic<ref name="negtc" /> and [[Jedi Order/Legends|Jedi Order]] to intervene.<ref name="nec" />

===Intervention===
[[File:IronKnight-NEGD.jpg|thumb|right|90px|The Iron Knights fought on behalf of the Republic forces.]]
The Republic eventually acquiesced to the Arkanians' entreaty, consenting to send Republic police squads and a number of [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]], including a young [[Mace Windu/Legends|Mace Windu]],<ref name="negtc" /> the [[Padawan/Legends|Padawan]] [[Squire Kinning]],<ref name="JP" /> as well as [[Aqinos]], a heterodox [[Sunesi]] [[Jedi Master/Legends|Jedi Master]]. Aqinos had controversially spent the previous ten years training a group of [[Force-sensitive/Legends|Force-sensitive]] [[Shard (species)|Shards]] to use the Force as Jedi, and these, encased within [[Uulshos justice droid]] bodies and known as the [[Iron Knight]]s,<ref name="datf">{{Hyperspace|url=hyperspace/member/insideronline/81/indexp7.html|text=Droids, Technology and the Force: A Clash of Phenomena|int=Droids, Technology and the Force: A Clash of Phenomena (article)}}</ref> accompanied him to Arkania.<ref name="negtd" />

The arrival of the combined Jedi/Republic task force quickly turned the tide in the conflict, though Kinning lost her arm in battle.<ref name="JP" /> Mace Windu did battle with Gorm, though both individuals survived.<ref name="negtc" /> Eventually, the Arkanian Revolution was crushed with the eventual defeat of the mercenary army at the hands of the Jedi.<ref name="nec" /> The surviving Renegades were executed<ref name="egtd" /> and the Dominion remained in power. The ultimate death toll was high, with thousands of pro-Yaka-modification scientists, the Arkanian Renegades, and the majority of their Assimilator cyborg army dead.<ref name="nec" />

==Aftermath==
The surviving Assimilator cyborgs fled Arkania and spread out into the wider galaxy, with many pursuing a career in [[Bounty hunter/Legends|bounty hunting]]. Gorm became the most infamous of the cyborgs, quickly acquiring a fearsome reputation as well as the sobriquet "the Dissolver."<ref name="egtd" /> He was still in active bounty hunting service over [[11 ABY/Legends|sixty years]] after the Revolution,<ref name="de">''[[Star Wars: Dark Empire]]''</ref> having learned to update and maintain himself through the decades. It was believed that, assuming he continued to patch himself up as necessary, Gorm the Dissolver could in theory continue to exist indefinitely.<ref name="egtd" />

Once the revolution was put down, the Arkanian Dominion systematically destroyed their army of Abhorers. One Abhorer, the [[Clone template/Legends|prime clone]] [[Zeta Magnus]], escaped destruction and dismembered the hunters the Dominion sent after him. He then went on a destructive rampage hunting down and eating his techno-lord creators out of vengeance. After avenging his brothers, Magnus searched for new ways to quench his desire for violence and mental stimulation by touring the galaxy for thirty years. While gathering knowledge, Magnus also gathered followers and [[Slavery/Legends|slaves]] which he used to build his own [[Dark Worlds|kingdom]] in the [[Unknown Regions/Legends|Unknown Regions]]. It was during this time that Magnus discovered his creators had programmed an expiration timer into his [[DNA/Legends|DNA]] to ensure their seemingly perfect mutant army couldn't live forever. During his search for immortality, Zeta Magnus was approached by [[Palpatine/Legends|Darth Sidious]] who offered him the secret to eternal life in exchange for his fealty. Magnus accepted and helped bioengineer many weapons for Sidious' [[Confederacy of Independent Systems/Legends|Separatist]] minions. His acts of terror led to him being wanted by the Republic and hunted by the Jedi. During the early stages of the [[Clone Wars/Legends|Clone Wars]], Magnus sent a [[Zeta Magnus (clone)|clone]] of himself to subjugate the planet [[Skye/Legends|Marat V]]. After the clone was defeated by Jedi Knight [[Obi-Wan Kenobi/Legends|Obi-Wan Kenobi]] and Jedi Padawans [[Halagad Ventor]] and [[Anakin Skywalker/Legends|Anakin Skywalker]], the original Magnus released a plague on numerous Republic worlds.<ref name="swcw">''[[SkyeWalkers: A Clone Wars Story]]''</ref>  

Mace Windu went on to become one of the most famous Jedi of the era, ultimately becoming [[Master of the Order/Legends|Master of the Order]] until his death in [[19 BBY/Legends|19 BBY]]. During the early Clone Wars, Mace Windu, believing him to be the architect of the revolution and the [[Second Sith theory|second Sith]], hunted the sole surviving Abhorer, Zeta Magnus, on [[Dantooine/Legends|Dantooine]].<ref name="swcw">''[[SkyeWalkers: A Clone Wars Story]]''</ref> Squire Kinning was brought back to the [[Jedi Temple/Legends|Jedi Temple]] to heal. Once she recovered, the loss of her arm was considered by the [[Jedi High Council/Legends|Jedi High Council]] to be her passing of the [[Jedi Trials/Legends#Trial of the Flesh|Trial of the Flesh]], and she soon went on to become a Jedi Knight.<ref name="JP" /> Aqinos' future was less bright; he was excommunicated from the Order for his unauthorized training of the Iron Knights, though he ultimately joined [[Luke Skywalker/Legends|Luke Skywalker's]] [[New Jedi Order]] but perished in the [[Yuuzhan Vong War]].<ref name="cswe">''[[The Complete Star Wars Encyclopedia]]''</ref>

The Dominion remained in power, continuing their experiments and earning a reputation as "mad scientists" during the time of the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]].<ref name="cswe" /> Though the existence of the Arkanian Renegades was suppressed to the extent that few had heard of them,<ref name="egtd" /> knowledge of the Revolution itself was commonplace.<ref name="nec" /> The conflict greatly revived a public fear of droids originating in the much larger-scale [[Great Droid Revolution]] on [[Coruscant/Legends|Coruscant]] thousands of years before.<ref name="negtd" /><ref name="datf" /> The conflict was also the first in a string of local uprisings to afflict the Republic during this period, one of many factors that weakened it and ultimately laid the foundations for [[Palpatine/Legends|Palpatine's]] [[New Order/Legends|coup]] in [[19 BBY/Legends|19 BBY]].<ref name="nec" />

==Behind the scenes==
The Arkanian Revolution was created by [[Daniel Wallace]] for ''[[The Essential Guide to Droids]]'' ([[1999]]), tying into the backstory of bounty hunter Gorm the Dissolver from [[Tom Veitch]]'s [[Dark Empire 3|''Dark Empire'' 3]] ([[1992]]). [[Ann Margaret Lewis (real person)|Ann Margaret Lewis's]] [[The Essential Guide to Alien Species (real-world book)|''The Essential Guide to Alien Species'']] ([[2001]]) later briefly expanded upon the conflict, before Wallace made references to it in his [[The New Essential Guide to Characters|''New Essential Guide to Characters'']] ([[2002]]), [[The New Essential Chronology|''New Essential Chronology'']] ([[2005]]), and [[The New Essential Guide to Droids|''New Essential Guide to Droids'']] ([[2006]]). [[Abel G. Peña]] also referenced it in [[Droids, Technology and the Force: A Clash of Phenomena (article)|''Droids, Technology and the Force: A Clash of Phenomena'']], a [[Hyperspace: The Official Star Wars Fan Club|Hyperspace]] article published in [[2005]], and in the novella [[SkyeWalkers: A Clone Wars Story]] which was intended to be published on Hyperspace, but was later released on [[StarWars.com]] in [[2015]].
